data.title: As an engineering team lead, I want T1 and T2 kudos triggers to award points for pre-existing bug fixes and previously-failing test remediation backed by snapshot evidence
data.description:
As an engineering team lead, I need T1 (pre-existing bug fix with test evidence) and T2 (previously-failing test fixed) kudos triggers so that agents earn recognition for deliberate quality improvements backed by snapshot evidence.

DONE DEFINITIONS:
- T1 and T2 integrated as functions in commit-validate.sh (same integration point as Epic 1 T-series triggers — no new hook or lifecycle script needed)
- T1 conditions (all must pass for award): (a) commit message matches pattern "(Closes|Fixes|Refs)\s+[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}" (b) dso ticket show <id> confirms type=bug AND first-event timestamp predates snapshot.merge_base (c) git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD Python source files (exclude test_*.py) → .test-index reverse lookup → test in snapshot as failed|timeout or aggregate_status=failed|timeout (per S2 decision) (d) current commit transitions test to passed
- T1 source file identification: parse git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD for Python source files (not test_*.py); for each, look up .test-index for associated test files; check snapshot for those tests
- T1 diagnostic output: when 3 of 4 conditions met, emit WARNING message to stdout identifying which condition failed and what was found; aids debuggability
- T1 fail-open: dso ticket show failure (network/parse) → skip with warning; snapshot absent/parse error → skip with warning
- T2 conditions: snapshot aggregate_status = failed|timeout (resource_exhaustion explicitly excluded); commit transitions test run to passed; snapshot tests map empty → no-op, no award (documented behavior)
- Kudos emission: calls commit-validate.sh emit_kudos function (from Epic 1 Story A ledger infrastructure) with trigger_id T1|T2
- Unit tests cover: each T1 condition independently (pass/fail for each); T2 pass, T2 skip (empty snapshot), T2 no-op (resource_exhaustion), T1 partial-match diagnostic output

CONSIDERATIONS:
- [Blocker] K depends on Epic 1 Story A (b41b-0369) for ledger infrastructure (emit_kudos function)
- [Blocker] K depends on Story J (ef94-8be1) for snapshot infrastructure
- [Data model] T1/T2 snapshot schema uses S2 spike decision (90ac-2818); if aggregate-only, T2 detects full test suite transition failed→passed; if per-test, T2 detects specific test transition
